mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous 3-month increase in payroll employment (PAYEMS) and decline in unemployment (UNRATE) signals strong labor market dynamics that boost corporate earnings visibility, generating positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS rises > 100k and UNRATE falls > 0.2pp in 3 months
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in triggered instances show negative or flat performance (IC < 0.05) despite concurrent PAYEMS/UNRATE shifts.

mechanism_narrative: A 1-month rise in industrial production (INDPRO) above its 3-month moving average indicates improving manufacturing activity, which drives equity risk premiums higher through stronger corporate cash flow expectations.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases but 20-day SPX returns fall below 0.5% in 70%+ of triggered periods.

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month decline in housing starts (HOUST) below 800k signals weak consumer demand for durable goods, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days through reduced corporate revenue expectations.
failure_condition: 5-day SPX returns remain positive in 60%+ of instances where HOUST falls below 800k.

mechanism_narrative: A 1-month increase in CPI (CPIAUCSL) above 235 signals inflationary pressures that force the Fed to tighten policy, creating negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days through higher discount rates.
failure_condition: 20-day SPX returns exceed 1.5% in 60%+ of instances where CPIAUCSL exceeds 235.

mechanism_narrative: A 4-quarter decline in GDP (GDPC1) combined with rising real sales (RSAFS) indicates a paradoxical slowdown in aggregate demand despite persistent consumer spending, creating negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day SPX returns remain positive in 70%+ of cases where GDPC1 declines and RSAFS rises.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month increase in payroll employment (PAYEMS) without corresponding wage growth (CPIAUCSL) signals underemployment, creating positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days through improved corporate profit margins.
failure_condition: 20-day SPX returns fall below 0.3% in 60%+ of instances where PAYEMS rises and CPIAUCSL stagnates.

mechanism_narrative: A 1-month drop in industrial production (INDPRO) below 98.5 combined with rising housing starts (HOUST) indicates sectoral imbalances that create negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days through sector rotation risks.
failure_condition: 5-day SPX returns exceed 1% in 60%+ of cases where INDPRO falls and HOUST rises.
